audience.description: Beginners through early professional developers and analysts who need to design, query, change, secure, tune, and defend relational data systems.
entryKnowledge: Navigate the learning platform, edit text, and reason about rows and columns in a small table.
Use basic arithmetic, comparisons, and plain-language if/then reasoning.
deviceConstraints: All executable SQL labs run locally in a disposable browser SQLite worker; a physical keyboard is recommended for longer projects.
accessibilityAssumptions: All schemas, plans, tables, query results, and traces require structured text, keyboard operation, announced status, and non-color-only meaning.

placementEvidence: A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Navigate the learning platform, edit text, and reason about rows and columns in a small table.
A placement review or prior artifact must demonstrate this entry skill: Use basic arithmetic, comparisons, and plain-language if/then reasoning.
completionEvidence: Complete and defend all 4 stakeholder projects against their acceptance constraints and rubrics.
Pass the cumulative performance examination at or above 85 percent with changed-case evidence.

includes: CS2023 Data Management core and applied KA topics: lifecycle, systems, modeling, relational design, querying, processing, internals, security, analytics, and distribution
Runnable SQLite 3.49.1 labs with explicit PostgreSQL 18.4 and current SQLite 3.53.3 dialect boundaries
Relational algebra intuition, SQL queries and mutations, constraints, normalization, transactions, windows, views, triggers, indexes, plans, parameterization, privacy, backup, and production defense
excludes: Vendor administration certification, kernel-level DBMS implementation, and formal proofs beyond those needed to defend practical schema and query decisions
Treating browser SQLite behavior as universal SQL behavior

sources.title: ACM/IEEE-CS/AAAI Computer Science Curricula 2023: Data Management
sources.scope: Controls institutional breadth, learning outcomes, modeling, querying, processing, internals, security, privacy, analytics, and distributed-system coverage.

sources.title: SQLite SQL Language Documentation
sources.version: SQLite 3.53.3 current; browser labs pinned to sql.js SQLite 3.49.1
sources.scope: Controls executable browser syntax, expressions, query, DDL, DML, transaction, trigger, and dialect behavior.

sources.title: SQLite Foreign Key Support
sources.scope: Controls referential-integrity activation, actions, indexes, and engine-specific limitations.

sources.title: SQLite Query Planning and Window Function Documentation
sources.scope: Controls browser-lab plan evidence, index reasoning, sorting, covering indexes, windows, and frame behavior.

sources.title: PostgreSQL SQL Language and Tutorial
sources.scope: Controls production-grade relational semantics, SQL breadth, data definition, manipulation, queries, types, functions, concurrency, and portability comparisons.

sources.title: PostgreSQL Concurrency Control and Indexes
sources.scope: Controls transaction isolation, concurrency phenomena, locking, serialization, index types, access paths, and operational tradeoffs.

sources.title: OWASP SQL Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
sources.scope: Controls prepared statements, parameterization, allow-list validation, stored-procedure cautions, and least-privilege practice.

competencies.statement: Trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction.
misconceptions: Keeping every field forever is the safest data policy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace data through creation, processing, review, retention, retrieval, and defensible destruction.

competencies.statement: Compare a DBMS with files and spreadsheets using integrity, concurrency, independence, recovery, and query needs.
misconceptions: A database is just a larger spreadsheet.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare a DBMS with files and spreadsheets using integrity, concurrency, independence, recovery, and query needs.

competencies.statement: Trace a query through parser, planner, executor, storage, buffer, transaction, and recovery responsibilities.
misconceptions: The database executes SQL directly in the order it is typed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace a query through parser, planner, executor, storage, buffer, transaction, and recovery responsibilities.

competencies.statement: State the required result as a declarative relation while separating it from execution strategy.
misconceptions: SQL requires the author to prescribe every loop and access path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State the required result as a declarative relation while separating it from execution strategy.

competencies.statement: Classify data purpose, owner, sensitivity, quality risk, retention, and downstream harm before storage.
misconceptions: If collection is legal, every later use is automatically ethical.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify data purpose, owner, sensitivity, quality risk, retention, and downstream harm before storage.

competencies.statement: Explain relations, tuples, attributes, domains, degree, cardinality, and why row order is not relational meaning.
misconceptions: A table is defined by its visible row order.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain relations, tuples, attributes, domains, degree, cardinality, and why row order is not relational meaning.

competencies.statement: Distinguish superkeys, candidate keys, primary keys, alternate keys, and stable surrogate identifiers.
misconceptions: Any unique-looking field is a durable primary key.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish superkeys, candidate keys, primary keys, alternate keys, and stable surrogate identifiers.

competencies.statement: Model optional and required references with foreign keys and predict update or delete consequences.
misconceptions: A foreign-key value must be globally unique.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model optional and required references with foreign keys and predict update or delete consequences.

competencies.statement: Represent unknown, missing, inapplicable, and not-yet-known values without substituting misleading sentinels.
misconceptions: NULL is the same as zero, an empty string, or the word unknown.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent unknown, missing, inapplicable, and not-yet-known values without substituting misleading sentinels.

competencies.statement: Declare the one-row meaning and reject mixed-grain tables before writing queries.
misconceptions: A table can safely combine event, ticket, and monthly summary rows.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Declare the one-row meaning and reject mixed-grain tables before writing queries.

competencies.statement: Compose SELECT and FROM clauses that project only required columns from a named relation.
misconceptions: SELECT * is the clearest and most stable production contract.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compose SELECT and FROM clauses that project only required columns from a named relation.

competencies.statement: Use relation and column aliases to make provenance and output contracts unambiguous.
misconceptions: Aliases change the stored schema.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use relation and column aliases to make provenance and output contracts unambiguous.

competencies.statement: Build arithmetic, text, date, and CASE expressions while tracing input and output meaning.
misconceptions: Every expression is evaluated before rows are selected.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build arithmetic, text, date, and CASE expressions while tracing input and output meaning.

competencies.statement: Distinguish implicit affinity from explicit conversion and test malformed or lossy values.
misconceptions: The database always converts text to numbers safely.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish implicit affinity from explicit conversion and test malformed or lossy values.

competencies.statement: Use duplicate-preserving and duplicate-eliminating results intentionally and explain their cost and semantics.
misconceptions: Duplicate output always proves duplicate stored rows.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use duplicate-preserving and duplicate-eliminating results intentionally and explain their cost and semantics.

competencies.statement: Combine comparison, range, membership, and boolean predicates with explicit grouping.
misconceptions: AND and OR can be mixed safely without parentheses.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine comparison, range, membership, and boolean predicates with explicit grouping.

competencies.statement: Predict TRUE, FALSE, and UNKNOWN through comparisons and boolean operators involving NULL.
misconceptions: A comparison with NULL returns FALSE.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict TRUE, FALSE, and UNKNOWN through comparisons and boolean operators involving NULL.

competencies.statement: Use IS NULL, IS NOT NULL, COALESCE, and NULLIF only when their business meaning is justified.
misconceptions: Equals NULL is the normal missing-value test.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use IS NULL, IS NOT NULL, COALESCE, and NULLIF only when their business meaning is justified.

competencies.statement: Apply LIKE and explicit escaping or case rules without confusing substring search with equality.
misconceptions: LIKE automatically provides portable case-insensitive search.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply LIKE and explicit escaping or case rules without confusing substring search with equality.

competencies.statement: Create deterministic multi-key ordering and evaluate offset and keyset pagination tradeoffs.
misconceptions: LIMIT alone guarantees repeatable page membership.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Create deterministic multi-key ordering and evaluate offset and keyset pagination tradeoffs.

competencies.statement: Use COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, and MAX while predicting empty-set, NULL, and type behavior.
misconceptions: COUNT(column) and COUNT(*) always count the same rows.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, and MAX while predicting empty-set, NULL, and type behavior.

competencies.statement: Set result grain with GROUP BY and verify that every selected value is grouped or aggregated.
misconceptions: GROUP BY merely sorts similar rows together.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set result grain with GROUP BY and verify that every selected value is grouped or aggregated.

competencies.statement: Separate row filtering in WHERE from group filtering in HAVING.
misconceptions: HAVING is a slower spelling of WHERE.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate row filtering in WHERE from group filtering in HAVING.

competencies.statement: Produce multiple defensible measures with CASE or FILTER while preserving one clear denominator.
misconceptions: A CASE expression inside an aggregate cannot change a metric denominator.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Produce multiple defensible measures with CASE or FILTER while preserving one clear denominator.

competencies.statement: Reconcile totals, denominators, missing groups, and changed cases before publishing an aggregate claim.
misconceptions: A plausible dashboard total is sufficient validation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconcile totals, denominators, missing groups, and changed cases before publishing an aggregate claim.

competencies.statement: Write explicit inner joins from relationship predicates and predict result cardinality before execution.
misconceptions: JOIN automatically infers the correct relationship.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write explicit inner joins from relationship predicates and predict result cardinality before execution.

competencies.statement: Use left, right, or full outer-join reasoning to retain required unmatched entities.
misconceptions: A WHERE predicate on the optional side cannot change an outer join.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use left, right, or full outer-join reasoning to retain required unmatched entities.

competencies.statement: Model and query many-to-many relationships through an associative relation with its own grain.
misconceptions: A comma-separated identifier list is a relational many-to-many design.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model and query many-to-many relationships through an associative relation with its own grain.

competencies.statement: Use self and cross joins only with explicit pair semantics and bounded cardinality.
misconceptions: A self join compares each row only with itself.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use self and cross joins only with explicit pair semantics and bounded cardinality.

competencies.statement: Detect join fanout with pre/post counts and repair aggregation at the correct grain.
misconceptions: DISTINCT is a safe universal repair for duplicated join results.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect join fanout with pre/post counts and repair aggregation at the correct grain.

competencies.statement: Choose scalar, row, and table subqueries whose cardinality matches their surrounding expression.
misconceptions: Every subquery may return any number of rows.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose scalar, row, and table subqueries whose cardinality matches their surrounding expression.

competencies.statement: Distinguish correlated subqueries and use EXISTS or NOT EXISTS for semijoin and antijoin questions.
misconceptions: NOT IN is always equivalent to NOT EXISTS when NULL appears.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish correlated subqueries and use EXISTS or NOT EXISTS for semijoin and antijoin questions.

competencies.statement: Use CTEs to name query stages while distinguishing readability from materialization guarantees.
misconceptions: A CTE always creates a temporary indexed table.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use CTEs to name query stages while distinguishing readability from materialization guarantees.

competencies.statement: Define anchor, recursive step, termination, and cycle or depth safeguards for hierarchical queries.
misconceptions: A recursive CTE stops automatically when the desired row appears.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define anchor, recursive step, termination, and cycle or depth safeguards for hierarchical queries.

competencies.statement: Use UNION, UNION ALL, INTERSECT, and EXCEPT with compatible columns and deliberate duplicate semantics.
misconceptions: UNION ALL and JOIN combine relations in the same way.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use UNION, UNION ALL, INTERSECT, and EXCEPT with compatible columns and deliberate duplicate semantics.

competencies.statement: Insert validated rows from explicit values or a query while naming target columns.
misconceptions: Omitting the target column list remains safe as schemas evolve.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Insert validated rows from explicit values or a query while naming target columns.

competencies.statement: Preview an UPDATE predicate as a SELECT and verify changed-row count and postconditions.
misconceptions: An UPDATE without a WHERE clause is easy to undo later.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preview an UPDATE predicate as a SELECT and verify changed-row count and postconditions.

competencies.statement: Distinguish row deletion, truncation, and retention-safe archival while predicting referential effects.
misconceptions: Deleting a parent can never affect related rows.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish row deletion, truncation, and retention-safe archival while predicting referential effects.

competencies.statement: Use a named uniqueness conflict and explicit update policy for idempotent ingestion.
misconceptions: Upsert means overwrite every existing column.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use a named uniqueness conflict and explicit update policy for idempotent ingestion.

competencies.statement: Capture affected identity, before/after evidence, actor, reason, and changed count for mutations.
misconceptions: A successful command message proves the correct rows changed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Capture affected identity, before/after evidence, actor, reason, and changed count for mutations.

competencies.statement: Derive entities, attributes, identifiers, relationships, optionality, and business rules from stakeholder language.
misconceptions: Every noun in a requirement becomes its own table.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive entities, attributes, identifiers, relationships, optionality, and business rules from stakeholder language.

competencies.statement: Model one-to-one, one-to-many, and many-to-many relationships with minimum and maximum cardinality.
misconceptions: A foreign key alone documents every optionality rule.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model one-to-one, one-to-many, and many-to-many relationships with minimum and maximum cardinality.

competencies.statement: Identify functional dependencies and candidate keys from semantics rather than sample coincidence.
misconceptions: If two sample columns match uniquely, one functionally determines the other.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify functional dependencies and candidate keys from semantics rather than sample coincidence.

competencies.statement: Transform repeating or partially dependent data through 1NF, 2NF, 3NF, and BCNF reasoning.
misconceptions: Normalization means creating as many tables as possible.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Transform repeating or partially dependent data through 1NF, 2NF, 3NF, and BCNF reasoning.

competencies.statement: Defend decomposition with lossless-join and dependency-preservation evidence and justify bounded denormalization.
misconceptions: Any decomposition that removes duplication is automatically correct.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend decomposition with lossless-join and dependency-preservation evidence and justify bounded denormalization.

competencies.statement: Define tables with deliberate types, defaults, generated values, and named constraints.
misconceptions: Choosing a broad text type eliminates domain design work.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define tables with deliberate types, defaults, generated values, and named constraints.

competencies.statement: Enforce NOT NULL, UNIQUE, CHECK, primary-key, and foreign-key invariants at the database boundary.
misconceptions: Application validation makes database constraints redundant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce NOT NULL, UNIQUE, CHECK, primary-key, and foreign-key invariants at the database boundary.

competencies.statement: Choose restrict, cascade, set-null, or set-default actions from lifecycle ownership semantics.
misconceptions: Cascade delete is the cleanest default for every relationship.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose restrict, cascade, set-null, or set-default actions from lifecycle ownership semantics.

competencies.statement: Plan backward-compatible expand, backfill, verify, switch, and contract phases with rollback evidence.
misconceptions: A migration is safe if its DDL succeeds on an empty database.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Plan backward-compatible expand, backfill, verify, switch, and contract phases with rollback evidence.

competencies.statement: Profile legacy violations, quarantine ambiguity, repair deterministically, and validate constraints before enforcement.
misconceptions: Invalid legacy data should be silently coerced during migration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Profile legacy violations, quarantine ambiguity, repair deterministically, and validate constraints before enforcement.

competencies.statement: Explain atomicity, consistency, isolation, and durability using a multi-statement business invariant.
misconceptions: ACID means every transaction runs one at a time.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain atomicity, consistency, isolation, and durability using a multi-statement business invariant.

competencies.statement: Bound a unit of work with BEGIN, COMMIT, ROLLBACK, and savepoints and verify failure behavior.
misconceptions: Errors always roll back every earlier statement automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound a unit of work with BEGIN, COMMIT, ROLLBACK, and savepoints and verify failure behavior.

competencies.statement: Recognize dirty read, nonrepeatable read, phantom, lost update, and write-skew schedules.
misconceptions: A transaction prevents all concurrency anomalies at every isolation level.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Recognize dirty read, nonrepeatable read, phantom, lost update, and write-skew schedules.

competencies.statement: Compare locking, multiversion, optimistic, and serialization-retry strategies from workload evidence.
misconceptions: Retrying any failed transaction is always safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare locking, multiversion, optimistic, and serialization-retry strategies from workload evidence.

competencies.statement: Distinguish rollback, crash recovery, backup, replication, and tested point-in-time restoration.
misconceptions: A replica is automatically a complete backup.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish rollback, crash recovery, backup, replication, and tested point-in-time restoration.

competencies.statement: Define window partition, order, and frame independently from final result ordering.
misconceptions: ORDER BY inside OVER also sorts the final output.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define window partition, order, and frame independently from final result ordering.

competencies.statement: Choose ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, and NTILE with explicit tie semantics.
misconceptions: All ranking functions assign the same values when ties occur.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, and NTILE with explicit tie semantics.

competencies.statement: Use LAG, LEAD, FIRST_VALUE, and LAST_VALUE with deliberate ordering and frame behavior.
misconceptions: LAST_VALUE always means the final row in the partition.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use LAG, LEAD, FIRST_VALUE, and LAST_VALUE with deliberate ordering and frame behavior.

competencies.statement: Build running and rolling aggregates with explicit ROWS or RANGE frames and edge-case checks.
misconceptions: The default frame is portable and always matches a running total.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build running and rolling aggregates with explicit ROWS or RANGE frames and edge-case checks.

competencies.statement: Separate instants, local civil time, duration, calendar boundaries, and incomplete reporting windows.
misconceptions: A timestamp string without zone context identifies one universal instant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate instants, local civil time, duration, calendar boundaries, and incomplete reporting windows.

competencies.statement: Design views as named query contracts with explicit columns, ownership, and change policy.
misconceptions: A view stores an independent copy of its result by default.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design views as named query contracts with explicit columns, ownership, and change policy.

competencies.statement: Choose live views, materialized views, summary tables, and cache refresh strategies from freshness needs.
misconceptions: Materialized results stay current without refresh.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose live views, materialized views, summary tables, and cache refresh strategies from freshness needs.

competencies.statement: Predict trigger timing, row versus statement scope, recursion, ordering limits, and failure behavior.
misconceptions: Triggers are visible and run identically across SQL dialects.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Predict trigger timing, row versus statement scope, recursion, ordering limits, and failure behavior.

competencies.statement: Place constraints, derived data, transitions, and orchestration in database or application layers deliberately.
misconceptions: All business logic belongs either entirely in SQL or entirely in application code.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Place constraints, derived data, transitions, and orchestration in database or application layers deliberately.

competencies.statement: Test trigger side effects, recursion guards, changed rows, rollback, and accessible audit evidence.
misconceptions: If the initiating statement succeeds, hidden trigger effects need no separate test.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test trigger side effects, recursion guards, changed rows, rollback, and accessible audit evidence.

competencies.statement: Explain B-tree and hash-style index strengths, ordering, lookup, storage, and update tradeoffs.
misconceptions: An index is a free sorted copy of the whole table.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain B-tree and hash-style index strengths, ordering, lookup, storage, and update tradeoffs.

competencies.statement: Order composite keys from predicates and sorting needs and identify prefix and covering behavior.
misconceptions: A composite index works equally well from any column position.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Order composite keys from predicates and sorting needs and identify prefix and covering behavior.

competencies.statement: Estimate selectivity and compare scans, seeks, lookups, and join access paths from data distribution.
misconceptions: An indexed predicate always uses its index.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Estimate selectivity and compare scans, seeks, lookups, and join access paths from data distribution.

competencies.statement: Read EXPLAIN evidence while separating estimates, actual measurements, and engine-specific plan vocabulary.
misconceptions: A lower estimated cost proves a query is faster in production.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Read EXPLAIN evidence while separating estimates, actual measurements, and engine-specific plan vocabulary.

competencies.statement: Benchmark representative warmed and cold cases, verify unchanged results, and account for write amplification.
misconceptions: One faster timing on a tiny fixture proves an optimization.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Benchmark representative warmed and cold cases, verify unchanged results, and account for write amplification.

competencies.statement: Identify code-data boundary failures in dynamic SQL and demonstrate why escaping alone is incomplete.
misconceptions: SQL injection is prevented by removing apostrophes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify code-data boundary failures in dynamic SQL and demonstrate why escaping alone is incomplete.

competencies.statement: Bind untrusted values through prepared statements while allow-listing unavoidable identifiers or directions.
misconceptions: Parameters can safely replace table names and SQL keywords.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind untrusted values through prepared statements while allow-listing unavoidable identifiers or directions.

competencies.statement: Design separate identities, roles, grants, views, and row or column controls for minimum required authority.
misconceptions: The application needs owner or administrator access to run migrations and queries.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design separate identities, roles, grants, views, and row or column controls for minimum required authority.

competencies.statement: Minimize collected fields, purpose, access, retention, export, and re-identification risk across the lifecycle.
misconceptions: Hashing a direct identifier automatically anonymizes a dataset.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Minimize collected fields, purpose, access, retention, export, and re-identification risk across the lifecycle.

competencies.statement: Record actor, authority, purpose, input provenance, query or change identity, and tamper-evident outcomes without logging secrets.
misconceptions: Logging every SQL statement with all values is the safest audit policy.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record actor, authority, purpose, input provenance, query or change identity, and tamper-evident outcomes without logging secrets.

competencies.statement: Separate standard relational intent from SQLite and PostgreSQL syntax, typing, NULL, date, and feature behavior.
misconceptions: SQL behaves identically across all conforming databases.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate standard relational intent from SQLite and PostgreSQL syntax, typing, NULL, date, and feature behavior.

competencies.statement: Choose relational, document, key-value, graph, or mixed models from access, integrity, evolution, and consistency needs.
misconceptions: NoSQL is automatically faster and more scalable than relational storage.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose relational, document, key-value, graph, or mixed models from access, integrity, evolution, and consistency needs.

competencies.statement: Explain replication, partitioning, distributed transactions, consistency choices, and failure boundaries.
misconceptions: Adding replicas creates unlimited write scale with no consistency tradeoff.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain replication, partitioning, distributed transactions, consistency choices, and failure boundaries.

competencies.statement: Define correctness, latency, saturation, lock, plan-regression, backup, and data-quality signals with response thresholds.
misconceptions: Database monitoring is complete when CPU and disk are graphed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define correctness, latency, saturation, lock, plan-regression, backup, and data-quality signals with response thresholds.

competencies.statement: Defend schema, queries, mutations, transactions, security, performance, portability, recovery, and ethical limits under changed evidence.
misconceptions: Passing the original demo proves the database system is production ready.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend schema, queries, mutations, transactions, security, performance, portability, recovery, and ethical limits under changed evidence.

projects.title: Production Data System Defense
projects.userNeed: The organization needs a portable data system plan with safe access, least privilege, recovery, observability, and justified storage choices.
constraints: Every input, assumption, transformation, and result is traceable.
Invalid, boundary, and changed cases are tested instead of silently discarded.
The result is understandable and useful to its named stakeholder.

assessmentBlueprint.formativeCorrectionPolicy: Failed evidence routes to the named misconception, one bounded corrective activity, and a parallel changed-case retry before progression.
